ARTICLE Reçu le 25 septembre 2016 | Accepté le 30 mai 2017 | Publié le 14 juillet 2017 DOI : 10.1038/ncomms16107OPEN Effet de l'activité de l'eau sur les taux de serpentinisation de l'olivine Hector M. Lamadrid1,2, J. Donald Rimstidt1, Esther M. Schwarzenbach3, Frieder Klein4, Sarah Ulrich1, Andrei Dolocan5 & Robert J. Bodnar1 L' altération hydrothermale des roches mantelliques (appelée serpentinisation) se produit en milieux sous-marins s'étendant des dorsales médio-océaniques aux zones de subduction.

tu.prop: ARTICLEReceived 25 Sep 2016 | Accepted 30 May 2017 | Published 14 Jul 2017DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107OPENEffect of water activity on rates of serpentinizationof olivineHector M. Lamadrid1,2, J. Donald Rimstidt1, Esther M. Schwarzenbach3, Frieder Klein4, Sarah Ulrich1,Andrei Dolocan5 & Robert J. Bodnar1The hydrothermal alteration of mantle rocks (referred to as serpentinization) occurs insubmarine environments extending from mid-ocean ridges to subduction zones.
In spite of increasing interest in the serpentinization processby researchers in diverse ﬁelds, the rates of serpentinization and the controlling factorsare poorly understood.

tuv.seg: Serpentini-zation affects the physical and chemical properties of oceanic lithosphere, represents one ofthe major mechanisms driving mass exchange between the mantle and the Earth’s surface,and is central to current origin of life hypotheses as well as the search for microbial life on theicy moons of Jupiter and Saturn.

La serpentinisation affecte les propriétés physiques et chimiques de la lithosphère océanique, et représente l'un des principaux mécanismes d'échange de masse entre le manteau et la surface de la Terre. Elle est également au cœur des hypothèses actuelles sur l'origine de la vie ainsi que de la recherche de la vie microbienne sur les lunes glacées de Jupiter et de Saturne.

tu.prop: Serpentini-zation affects the physical and chemical properties of oceanic lithosphere, represents one ofthe major mechanisms driving mass exchange between the mantle and the Earth’s surface,and is central to current origin of life hypotheses as well as the search for microbial life on theicy moons of Jupiter and Saturn.

Here we use a novel in situ experimental method involving olivinemicro-reactors and show that the rate of serpentinization is strongly controlled by the salinity(water activity) of the reacting ﬂuid and demonstrate that the rate of serpentinization ofolivine slows down as salinity increases and H2O activity decreases.1 Department of Geosciences, Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, Virginia 24061, USA.

tuv.seg: In spite of increasing interest in the serpentinization processby researchers in diverse ﬁelds, the rates of serpentinization and the controlling factorsare poorly understood.
Malgré un intérêt croissant des chercheurs dans divers domaines, pour le processus de serpentinisation, les taux de serpentinisation et les facteurs de contrôle sont mal compris.

tu.prop: In spite of increasing interest in the serpentinization processby researchers in diverse ﬁelds, the rates of serpentinization and the controlling factorsare poorly understood.
2 Department of Earth Sciences, University of Toronto, 22 Russell Street,Toronto, Ontario, Canada M5S 3B1.

tuv.seg: Here we use a novel in situ experimental method involving olivinemicro-reactors and show that the rate of serpentinization is strongly controlled by the salinity(water activity) of the reacting ﬂuid and demonstrate that the rate of serpentinization ofolivine slows down as salinity increases and H2O activity decreases.1 Department of Geosciences, Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, Virginia 24061, USA.

Nous utilisons ici une nouvelle méthode expérimentale in situ impliquant des olivines micro-réacteurs et montrons que le taux de serpentinisation est fortement contrôlé par la salinité (activité de l'eau) du ﬂuide de réaction. Nous démontrons également que le taux de serpentinisation de l'olivine ralentit à mesure que la salinité augmente et que l'activité de l'eau diminue. 1 Department of Geosciences, Virginia Tech, Blacksburg, Virginia 24061, États-Unis.

tuv.seg: NATURE COMMUNICATIONS | 8:16107 | DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107 | www.nature.com/naturecommunications1
NATURE COMMUNICATIONS | 8:16107 | DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107 | www.nature.com/naturecommunications1

tu.prop: Serpentinization affects the chemical composition, rheology,magnetic properties, seismic structure and habitability of theshallow lithosphere at slow- and ultraslow-spreading mid-oceanridges, continental margins and forearc settings of subductionzones2–8.

tuv.seg: ARTICLENATURE COMMUNICATIONS | DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107lowerSerpentinization encompasses a series of hydration reactionsthat occur when ultramaﬁc rocks are exposed to circulatingthan B400 (cid:2)C,temperaturesaqueous ﬂuids atleading to the formation of serpentine phases (lizardite andchrysotile)±brucite±talc±magnetite, among other minerals1.

La serpentinisation englobe une série de réactions d'hydratation qui se produisent lorsque des roches ultramafiques sont exposées à des fluides aqueux circulant à des températures inférieures à ∼400 C, entraînant la formation de phases serpentines (lizardite et chrysotile) ± brucite ± talc ± magnétite, entre autres minéraux.1

tu.prop: ARTICLENATURE COMMUNICATIONS | DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107lowerSerpentinization encompasses a series of hydration reactionsthat occur when ultramaﬁc rocks are exposed to circulatingthan B400 (cid:2)C,temperaturesaqueous ﬂuids atleading to the formation of serpentine phases (lizardite andchrysotile)±brucite±talc±magnetite, among other minerals1.
Serpentinization also inﬂuences subduction relatedprocesses9 and the geochemical cycling of volatile species (that is,H2O, CO2 and H2S) and ﬂuid-mobile elements10–13.

tuv.seg: Serpentinization affects the chemical composition, rheology,magnetic properties, seismic structure and habitability of theshallow lithosphere at slow- and ultraslow-spreading mid-oceanridges, continental margins and forearc settings of subductionzones2–8.

La serpentinisation affecte la composition chimique, la rhéologie, les propriétés magnétiques, la structure sismique et l'habitabilité de la lithosphère peu profonde dans les dorsales médio-océaniques lentes et ultra-lentes, les marges continentales et les paramètres d'avant-arc des zones de subduction2-8.

tu.prop: Serpentinization affects the chemical composition, rheology,magnetic properties, seismic structure and habitability of theshallow lithosphere at slow- and ultraslow-spreading mid-oceanridges, continental margins and forearc settings of subductionzones2–8.

Recentﬁndings suggest that serpentinization of olivine-rich lithologiesalso takes place on other planetary bodies, such as the icy moonsof Jupiter and Saturn, which in turn, has important implicationsconcerning their habitability14,15.

tuv.seg: Serpentinization also inﬂuences subduction relatedprocesses9 and the geochemical cycling of volatile species (that is,H2O, CO2 and H2S) and ﬂuid-mobile elements10–13.
La serpentinisation influence également les processus liés à la subduction9 et le cycle géochimique des espèces volatiles (c'est-à-dire le H2O, le CO2 et le H2S) et des éléments mobiles fluides10-13.

tu.prop: Serpentinization also inﬂuences subduction relatedprocesses9 and the geochemical cycling of volatile species (that is,H2O, CO2 and H2S) and ﬂuid-mobile elements10–13.

Despite the pivotal role that serpentinization plays in a numberof geological and biological processes and its central role incurrent origin of life hypotheses16–19, few experimental studieshave attempted to determine the rates ofserpentinizationreactions and the ratesthat have been reported divergewidely20–25.

tuv.seg: Recentﬁndings suggest that serpentinization of olivine-rich lithologiesalso takes place on other planetary bodies, such as the icy moonsof Jupiter and Saturn, which in turn, has important implicationsconcerning their habitability14,15.

Des découvertes récentes suggèrent que la serpentinisation des lithologies riches en olivine a également lieu sur d'autres corps planétaires, tels que les lunes glacées de Jupiter et de Saturne, ce qui a d'importantes implications sur leur habitabilité14, 15.

tu.prop: Recentﬁndings suggest that serpentinization of olivine-rich lithologiesalso takes place on other planetary bodies, such as the icy moonsof Jupiter and Saturn, which in turn, has important implicationsconcerning their habitability14,15.
Furthermore, the environmental factors that affectthe reaction rates are incompletely constrained.

tuv.seg: Despite the pivotal role that serpentinization plays in a numberof geological and biological processes and its central role incurrent origin of life hypotheses16–19, few experimental studieshave attempted to determine the rates ofserpentinizationreactions and the ratesthat have been reported divergewidely20–25.

Malgré le rôle central que joue la serpentinisation dans un certain nombre de processus géologiques et biologiques et son rôle central dans les hypothèses de l'origine de la vie16-19, peu d'études expérimentales ont tenté de déterminer les taux de réactions de serpentinisation, et les taux qui ont été communiqués divergent largement20-25.

tu.prop: Despite the pivotal role that serpentinization plays in a numberof geological and biological processes and its central role incurrent origin of life hypotheses16–19, few experimental studieshave attempted to determine the rates ofserpentinizationreactions and the ratesthat have been reported divergewidely20–25.
In the presentwork, we used synthetic ﬂuid inclusions (SFIs)26,27 as micro-reactors in olivine crystals to monitor the serpentinizationreaction with time.

tuv.seg: Furthermore, the environmental factors that affectthe reaction rates are incompletely constrained.
De plus, les facteurs environnementaux qui affectent le taux de réaction sont contraints de façon incomplète.

tu.prop: Furthermore, the environmental factors that affectthe reaction rates are incompletely constrained.
We trapped ﬂuid with differentinitialsalinities and followed reaction progress at serpentinizationconditions (280 (cid:2)C).

tuv.seg: In the presentwork, we used synthetic ﬂuid inclusions (SFIs)26,27 as micro-reactors in olivine crystals to monitor the serpentinizationreaction with time.

Dans ce travail, nous avons utilisé des inclusions de fluides synthétiques (IFS)26-27 comme microréacteurs dans des cristaux d'olivine pour surveiller la réaction de serpentinisation au cours du temps.

tu.prop: In the presentwork, we used synthetic ﬂuid inclusions (SFIs)26,27 as micro-reactors in olivine crystals to monitor the serpentinizationreaction with time.
The results show that the rates of olivineserpentinization are strongly inﬂuenced by the aqueous ﬂuidsalinity.

tuv.seg: We trapped ﬂuid with differentinitialsalinities and followed reaction progress at serpentinizationconditions (280 (cid:2)C).
Nous avons piégé le fluide avec différentes salinités initiales et suivi les progrès de la réaction dans des conditions de serpentinisation (280 °C).

tu.prop: We trapped ﬂuid with differentinitialsalinities and followed reaction progress at serpentinizationconditions (280 (cid:2)C).
The micro-reactor technique presents several advantagesand permits monitoring mineral precipitation and water activityin situ and in real time.ResultsSFI preparation.

tuv.seg: The results show that the rates of olivineserpentinization are strongly inﬂuenced by the aqueous ﬂuidsalinity.
Les résultats montrent que les taux de serpentinisation de l'olivine sont fortement influencés par la salinité des fluides aqueux.

tu.prop: The results show that the rates of olivineserpentinization are strongly inﬂuenced by the aqueous ﬂuidsalinity.
We trapped SFIs26,27 in gem quality (inclusion-free) natural olivine at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar, conditions whereolivine and H2O coexist stably (Fig. 1).

tuv.seg: The micro-reactor technique presents several advantagesand permits monitoring mineral precipitation and water activityin situ and in real time.ResultsSFI preparation.
La technique du microréacteur présente plusieurs avantages et permet de surveiller la précipitation minérale et l'activité de l'eau in situ et en temps réel. Résultats Préparation des IFS.

tu.prop: The micro-reactor technique presents several advantagesand permits monitoring mineral precipitation and water activityin situ and in real time.ResultsSFI preparation.
The starting H2O-NaCl-MgCl2 ﬂuids in each experiment had a Na/Mg molar ratio similarto that of seawater (8/1) and total salinities of 1, 3.5, 6 and10 wt%.

tuv.seg: We trapped SFIs26,27 in gem quality (inclusion-free) natural olivine at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar, conditions whereolivine and H2O coexist stably (Fig. 1).
Nous avons piégé des IFS26-27 dans de l'olivine naturelle de qualité gemme (sans inclusion) à 650 °C et 5,5 kbar, les conditions où l'olivine et l'H2O coexistent de manière stable (Fig. 1).

tu.prop: We trapped SFIs26,27 in gem quality (inclusion-free) natural olivine at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar, conditions whereolivine and H2O coexist stably (Fig. 1).

The capsules were sealed with an arc welder and thenplaced into cold-seal pressure vessels and held at the desiredtrapping conditions (650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar) for 30 days, whichallowed the olivine host to anneal and trap some of the ﬂuid asSFIs.

tuv.seg: The starting H2O-NaCl-MgCl2 ﬂuids in each experiment had a Na/Mg molar ratio similarto that of seawater (8/1) and total salinities of 1, 3.5, 6 and10 wt%.
Les fluides de départ H2O-NaCl- MgCl2 de chaque expérience avaient un rapport molaire Na/Mg similaire à celui de l'eau de mer (8/1) et des salinités totales de 1, 3,5, 6 et 10 % en poids.

tu.prop: The starting H2O-NaCl-MgCl2 ﬂuids in each experiment had a Na/Mg molar ratio similarto that of seawater (8/1) and total salinities of 1, 3.5, 6 and10 wt%.

The P–T conditions of trapping of the SFI were chosen suchthe trapped ﬂuid would have a density B0.8 g cm (cid:2) 3.thatFollowing inclusion synthesis, doubly polished wafers wereprepared and individual wafers were placed into a furnace at280 (cid:2)C and ambientthistemperature, olivine is no longer stable and will react with theaqueous solution to produce serpentinization.

tuv.seg: The capsules were sealed with an arc welder and thenplaced into cold-seal pressure vessels and held at the desiredtrapping conditions (650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar) for 30 days, whichallowed the olivine host to anneal and trap some of the ﬂuid asSFIs.

Les capsules ont été scellées avec une soudeuse à l'arc et ensuite placées dans des autoclaves à basse température et maintenues dans les conditions de piégeage désirées (650 °C et 5,5 kbar) pendant 30 jours, ce qui a permis à l'olivine hôte de recuire et de piéger une partie du fluide comme IFS.

tu.prop: The capsules were sealed with an arc welder and thenplaced into cold-seal pressure vessels and held at the desiredtrapping conditions (650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar) for 30 days, whichallowed the olivine host to anneal and trap some of the ﬂuid asSFIs.

At a temperature of280 (cid:2)C and constant volume of the SFI, the calculated pressureinside the SFI is B500 bar.external pressure(B1 bar)—atThe SFIs can be envisioned as micro-reactors, a reaction vesselin which material can only be exchanged between the aqueoussolution and the surrounding host olivine.

tuv.seg: The P–T conditions of trapping of the SFI were chosen suchthe trapped ﬂuid would have a density B0.8 g cm (cid:2) 3.thatFollowing inclusion synthesis, doubly polished wafers wereprepared and individual wafers were placed into a furnace at280 (cid:2)C and ambientthistemperature, olivine is no longer stable and will react with theaqueous solution to produce serpentinization.

Les conditions P–T de piégeage de l'IFS ont été choisies pour que le ﬂuide piégé ait une densité ∼0,8 g cm-3. Après la synthèse d'inclusion, les plaques doublement polies ont été préparées, et les plaques individuelles ont été placées dans un four à 280 °C et avec une pression externe ambiante. À cette température, l'olivine n'est plus stable et réagit avec la solution aqueuse pour produire la serpentinisation.

tu.prop: The P–T conditions of trapping of the SFI were chosen suchthe trapped ﬂuid would have a density B0.8 g cm (cid:2) 3.thatFollowing inclusion synthesis, doubly polished wafers wereprepared and individual wafers were placed into a furnace at280 (cid:2)C and ambientthistemperature, olivine is no longer stable and will react with theaqueous solution to produce serpentinization.

A major advantage ofthis approach over most other existing experimental techniques isthat changes in mineralogy and ﬂuid composition can bemonitored simultaneously in real time as the reaction progresses.Serpentinization experiments.

tuv.seg: At a temperature of280 (cid:2)C and constant volume of the SFI, the calculated pressureinside the SFI is B500 bar.external pressure(B1 bar)—atThe SFIs can be envisioned as micro-reactors, a reaction vesselin which material can only be exchanged between the aqueoussolution and the surrounding host olivine.

À une température de 280 °C et à un volume constant de l'IFS, la pression calculée à l'intérieur de l'IFS est de ∼500 bars. Les IFS peuvent être considérées comme des microréacteurs, une cellule de réaction dans laquelle la matière ne peut être échangée qu'entre la solution aqueuse et l'olivine hôte environnante.

tu.prop: At a temperature of280 (cid:2)C and constant volume of the SFI, the calculated pressureinside the SFI is B500 bar.external pressure(B1 bar)—atThe SFIs can be envisioned as micro-reactors, a reaction vesselin which material can only be exchanged between the aqueoussolution and the surrounding host olivine.
Following the formation of theSFI in olivine, samples were heated to 280 (cid:2)C and held at thistemperature for 30 to 270 days.

tuv.seg: A major advantage ofthis approach over most other existing experimental techniques isthat changes in mineralogy and ﬂuid composition can bemonitored simultaneously in real time as the reaction progresses.Serpentinization experiments.

Un avantage majeur de cette approche par rapport à la plupart des autres techniques expérimentales existantes est que les changements dans la minéralogie et la composition du fluide peuvent être suivis simultanément en temps réel au fur et à mesure que la réaction progresse. Expériences de serpentinisation.

tu.prop: A major advantage ofthis approach over most other existing experimental techniques isthat changes in mineralogy and ﬂuid composition can bemonitored simultaneously in real time as the reaction progresses.Serpentinization experiments.
For each ﬂuid composition andsalinity, several SFI in each olivine wafer were selected to monitorserpentinization reaction progress (Table 1).

tuv.seg: Following the formation of theSFI in olivine, samples were heated to 280 (cid:2)C and held at thistemperature for 30 to 270 days.
Suite à la formation de l'IFS dans l'olivine, les échantillons ont été chauffés à 280 °C et maintenus à cette température entre 30 et 270 jours.

tu.prop: Following the formation of theSFI in olivine, samples were heated to 280 (cid:2)C and held at thistemperature for 30 to 270 days.
Each inclusion wasphotographed and mapped, and each was regularly monitoredoptically, by microthermometry and by Raman spectroscopy.

tuv.seg: For each ﬂuid composition andsalinity, several SFI in each olivine wafer were selected to monitorserpentinization reaction progress (Table 1).
Pour chaque composition de fluide et salinité, plusieurs IFS dans chaque plaque d'olivine ont été sélectionnées pour suivre l'évolution de la réaction de serpentinisation (cf. Tableau 1).

tu.prop: For each ﬂuid composition andsalinity, several SFI in each olivine wafer were selected to monitorserpentinization reaction progress (Table 1).
Thepolished olivine wafers were brieﬂy (2–5 h) removed from thefurnace every 5 days, examined petrographically and analysed byRaman spectroscopy, and then returned to the furnace.

tuv.seg: Each inclusion wasphotographed and mapped, and each was regularly monitoredoptically, by microthermometry and by Raman spectroscopy.
Chaque inclusion a été photographiée et cartographiée, mais a aussi fait objet d'un suivi régulier par voie optique, par microthermométrie et spectroscopie Raman.

tu.prop: Each inclusion wasphotographed and mapped, and each was regularly monitoredoptically, by microthermometry and by Raman spectroscopy.
Figure 1(0 days) shows a typical SFI trapped in olivine immediately beforestarting the heating experiments to initiate the serpentinizationreaction.Reaction progress.

tuv.seg: Thepolished olivine wafers were brieﬂy (2–5 h) removed from thefurnace every 5 days, examined petrographically and analysed byRaman spectroscopy, and then returned to the furnace.

Tous les cinq jours, les plaquettes d'olivine polies ont été temporairement (entre 2 h et 5 h) retirées du four, examinées pétrographiquement et analysées par spectroscopie Raman, avant d'être remises dans le four.

tu.prop: Thepolished olivine wafers were brieﬂy (2–5 h) removed from thefurnace every 5 days, examined petrographically and analysed byRaman spectroscopy, and then returned to the furnace.

After a few days of reaction time, smallclusters of secondary minerals are observed in the SFI (Fig. 1, 15days) and the size and number of individual crystals in the SFIprogressively increased with time until the inclusions were nearlyﬁlled with solids, rendering them opaque (Fig. 1, 45 days).

tuv.seg: Figure 1(0 days) shows a typical SFI trapped in olivine immediately beforestarting the heating experiments to initiate the serpentinizationreaction.Reaction progress.

La figure 1 (0 jour) montre une inclusion de fluide synthétique typique, immédiatement piégé dans l'olivine, avant de commencer les expériences de chauffage pour initier la réaction de serpentinisation. Progression de la réaction.

tu.prop: Figure 1(0 days) shows a typical SFI trapped in olivine immediately beforestarting the heating experiments to initiate the serpentinizationreaction.Reaction progress.
The onset of the serpentinization reaction varied with thesalinity of the starting ﬂuid.

tuv.seg: After a few days of reaction time, smallclusters of secondary minerals are observed in the SFI (Fig. 1, 15days) and the size and number of individual crystals in the SFIprogressively increased with time until the inclusions were nearlyﬁlled with solids, rendering them opaque (Fig. 1, 45 days).

Au bout de quelques jours de réaction, de petits amas de minéraux secondaires sont observés dans l'IFS (cf. Fig. 1, 15 jours) et la taille et le nombre de cristaux individuels dans l'IFS ont progressivement augmenté jusqu'à ce que les inclusions soient presque remplies de solides, ce qui les rend opaques (cf. Fig. 1, 45 jours).

tu.prop: After a few days of reaction time, smallclusters of secondary minerals are observed in the SFI (Fig. 1, 15days) and the size and number of individual crystals in the SFIprogressively increased with time until the inclusions were nearlyﬁlled with solids, rendering them opaque (Fig. 1, 45 days).
Reaction products were ﬁrst observedafter 5 days in B50% of the SFIs with a starting salinity of 1 wt%.

tuv.seg: The onset of the serpentinization reaction varied with thesalinity of the starting ﬂuid.
Le début de la réaction de serpentinisation variait avec la salinité du fluide de départ.

tu.prop: The onset of the serpentinization reaction varied with thesalinity of the starting ﬂuid.
The proportion of the SFI showing evidence of reaction after5 days decreased to B20, B5 and 0% in the SFI with startingsalinities of 3.5, 6 and 10 wt%, respectively.

tuv.seg: Reaction products were ﬁrst observedafter 5 days in B50% of the SFIs with a starting salinity of 1 wt%.
Les produits de réaction ont été observés pour la première fois après 5 jours dans ∼50 % des IFS avec une salinité de départ de 1 %.

tu.prop: Reaction products were ﬁrst observedafter 5 days in B50% of the SFIs with a starting salinity of 1 wt%.
At low salinities theproportion of SFI showing reaction products increased rapidlywith time until they were ubiquitous in the SFI.

tuv.seg: The proportion of the SFI showing evidence of reaction after5 days decreased to B20, B5 and 0% in the SFI with startingsalinities of 3.5, 6 and 10 wt%, respectively.
La proportion d'IFS montrant des signes de réaction après 5 jours a diminué à ∼20, ∼5 et 0 % dans l'IFS avec des salinités initiales de 3,5, 6 et 10 %, respectivement.

tu.prop: The proportion of the SFI showing evidence of reaction after5 days decreased to B20, B5 and 0% in the SFI with startingsalinities of 3.5, 6 and 10 wt%, respectively.

For the highestthe ﬁrst evidence of reactionsalinity experiments (10 wt%),product formation was observed only after 4120 days.In addition to the experiments in which samples were regularlyremoved from the furnace for observation and analysis, twosamples containing SFI with salinities of 1 and 3.5 wt% weremaintained at constant temperature using a Chaixmeca micro-scope heating stage28.

tuv.seg: At low salinities theproportion of SFI showing reaction products increased rapidlywith time until they were ubiquitous in the SFI.
À de faibles salinités, la proportion d'IFS montrant des produits de réaction a augmenté rapidement avec le temps jusqu'à ce qu'ils soient omniprésents dans l'IFS.

tu.prop: At low salinities theproportion of SFI showing reaction products increased rapidlywith time until they were ubiquitous in the SFI.
For these samples,the SFI could bemonitored without having to cool the sample from 280 (cid:2)C toroom temperature.

tuv.seg: For the highestthe ﬁrst evidence of reactionsalinity experiments (10 wt%),product formation was observed only after 4120 days.In addition to the experiments in which samples were regularlyremoved from the furnace for observation and analysis, twosamples containing SFI with salinities of 1 and 3.5 wt% weremaintained at constant temperature using a Chaixmeca micro-scope heating stage28.

Pour les expériences avec la salinité la plus élevée (10 %), les premiers signes de formation de produits de réaction n'ont été observés qu'après plus de 120 jours. En plus des expériences dans lesquelles les échantillons ont été régulièrement retirés du four pour être observés et analysés, deux échantillons contenant des IFS avec des salinités de 1 et 3,5 % ont été maintenus à une température constante à l'aide d'une platine chauffante pour microscope Chaixmeca28.

tu.prop: For the highestthe ﬁrst evidence of reactionsalinity experiments (10 wt%),product formation was observed only after 4120 days.In addition to the experiments in which samples were regularlyremoved from the furnace for observation and analysis, twosamples containing SFI with salinities of 1 and 3.5 wt% weremaintained at constant temperature using a Chaixmeca micro-scope heating stage28.

These experiments were conducted to conﬁrmthat repeatedly cycling the samples from 280 (cid:2)C to roomtemperature and back to 280 (cid:2)C did not signiﬁcantly affect theprogression of the experiments.

tuv.seg: For these samples,the SFI could bemonitored without having to cool the sample from 280 (cid:2)C toroom temperature.
Pour ces échantillons, l'IFS a pu être contrôlée sans qu'il soit nécessaire de refroidir l'échantillon de 280 C à la température ambiante.

tu.prop: For these samples,the SFI could bemonitored without having to cool the sample from 280 (cid:2)C toroom temperature.
No differences were observedbetween the thermal cycling experiments and the constant-temperature Chaixmeca experiments.Mineral characterization.

tuv.seg: These experiments were conducted to conﬁrmthat repeatedly cycling the samples from 280 (cid:2)C to roomtemperature and back to 280 (cid:2)C did not signiﬁcantly affect theprogression of the experiments.

Ces expériences ont été menées pour confirmer que le passage répété des échantillons de 280 C à la température ambiante, puis de nouveau à 280 C, n'affectait pas de manière significative la progression des expériences.

tu.prop: These experiments were conducted to conﬁrmthat repeatedly cycling the samples from 280 (cid:2)C to roomtemperature and back to 280 (cid:2)C did not signiﬁcantly affect theprogression of the experiments.

Raman spectroscopic analyses of theSFI (Fig. 2a,b) conﬁrmed that the reaction products consist ofserpentine minerals (Srp,lizardite and chrysotile) and brucite(Brc) produced according to the simpliﬁed reaction (note that Fecan substitute for Mg in all three mineral phases, but the reactionbelow is written in terms of the Mg endmembers):2Mg2SiO4 OlðÞ þ 3H2O , Mg3Si2O5 OHðÞ4 SrpðÞ þ Mg OHðÞÞ2 Brcðð1ÞMagnetite is a minor but common byproduct of serpentiniza-tion reactions.

tuv.seg: No differences were observedbetween the thermal cycling experiments and the constant-temperature Chaixmeca experiments.Mineral characterization.
Aucune différence n'a été observée entre les expériences de cyclage thermique et les expériences à température constante de Chaixmeca. Caractérisation minérale

tu.prop: No differences were observedbetween the thermal cycling experiments and the constant-temperature Chaixmeca experiments.Mineral characterization.

It forms when the Fe2 þ released from olivine isoxidized in the ﬂuid and precipitates magnetite and generates H2:3Fe2 þ þ 4H2O , Fe3O4 þ H2 þ 6H þð2ÞIn our experiments, magnetite was presentin detectableamounts only after 4120 days of reaction time.

tuv.seg: Raman spectroscopic analyses of theSFI (Fig. 2a,b) conﬁrmed that the reaction products consist ofserpentine minerals (Srp,lizardite and chrysotile) and brucite(Brc) produced according to the simpliﬁed reaction (note that Fecan substitute for Mg in all three mineral phases, but the reactionbelow is written in terms of the Mg endmembers):2Mg2SiO4 OlðÞ þ 3H2O , Mg3Si2O5 OHðÞ4 SrpðÞ þ Mg OHðÞÞ2 Brcðð1ÞMagnetite is a minor but common byproduct of serpentiniza-tion reactions.

Les analyses spectroscopiques Raman de l'IFS (cf. Fig. 2a,b) ont confirmé que les produits de la réaction sont constitués de minéraux de serpentine (Srp, lizardite et chrysotile) et de brucite (Brc) produits selon la réaction simplifiée (notez que le Fe peut se substituer au Mg dans les trois phases minérales, mais la réaction ci-dessous est écrite selon les termes extrêmes du Mg) : 2Mg2SiO4(Ol)+3H20 <-->Mg3Si205(OH)4(Srp)+Mg(OH)2(Brc) (1). La magnétite est un sous-produit mineur mais courant des réactions de serpentinisation.

tu.prop: Raman spectroscopic analyses of theSFI (Fig. 2a,b) conﬁrmed that the reaction products consist ofserpentine minerals (Srp,lizardite and chrysotile) and brucite(Brc) produced according to the simpliﬁed reaction (note that Fecan substitute for Mg in all three mineral phases, but the reactionbelow is written in terms of the Mg endmembers):2Mg2SiO4 OlðÞ þ 3H2O , Mg3Si2O5 OHðÞ4 SrpðÞ þ Mg OHðÞÞ2 Brcðð1ÞMagnetite is a minor but common byproduct of serpentiniza-tion reactions.

Small magnetitecrystals, formed via reaction (2) (but not detectable by opticalmicroscopy) should begin to precipitate inside the micro-reactorssoon after reaction (3) starts and the solubility of magnetite in thesolution is exceeded.

tuv.seg: It forms when the Fe2 þ released from olivine isoxidized in the ﬂuid and precipitates magnetite and generates H2:3Fe2 þ þ 4H2O , Fe3O4 þ H2 þ 6H þð2ÞIn our experiments, magnetite was presentin detectableamounts only after 4120 days of reaction time.

Elle se forme lorsque le Fe2+ libéré par l'olivine est oxydé dans le fluide, précipite la magnétite et génère du H2 : 3Fe2++4H2O<-->Fr3O4+H2+H6+ (2). Dans nos expériences, la magnétite n'était présente en quantités détectables qu'après plus de 120 jours de temps de réaction.

tu.prop: It forms when the Fe2 þ released from olivine isoxidized in the ﬂuid and precipitates magnetite and generates H2:3Fe2 þ þ 4H2O , Fe3O4 þ H2 þ 6H þð2ÞIn our experiments, magnetite was presentin detectableamounts only after 4120 days of reaction time.

However, we interpret the lack of observablemagnetite in the inclusions early in the experiments to reﬂect thefact that the crystals are not optically resolvable (B0.5 mm) by themethods used here (including scanning electron microscopy(SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) in Fig. 2c,d).

tuv.seg: Small magnetitecrystals, formed via reaction (2) (but not detectable by opticalmicroscopy) should begin to precipitate inside the micro-reactorssoon after reaction (3) starts and the solubility of magnetite in thesolution is exceeded.

De petits cristaux de magnétite, formés par réaction (2) (mais non détectables par microscopie optique) devraient commencer à précipiter à l'intérieur des microréacteurs peu après le début de la réaction (3) et le dépassement de la solubilité de la magnétite dans la solution.

tu.prop: Small magnetitecrystals, formed via reaction (2) (but not detectable by opticalmicroscopy) should begin to precipitate inside the micro-reactorssoon after reaction (3) starts and the solubility of magnetite in thesolution is exceeded.
In order for magnetite to become sufﬁciently large that they canbe recognized either optically or during microanalysis, hydrogenmust be lost from the SFI.

tuv.seg: However, we interpret the lack of observablemagnetite in the inclusions early in the experiments to reﬂect thefact that the crystals are not optically resolvable (B0.5 mm) by themethods used here (including scanning electron microscopy(SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) in Fig. 2c,d).

Cependant, nous interprétons l'absence de magnétite observable dans les inclusions au début des expériences comme reflétant le fait que les cristaux ne sont pas optiquement résolvables (∼0.5 μm) par les méthodes utilisées ici (y compris la microscopie électronique à balayage (MEB) et la microscopie électronique à transmission.

tu.prop: However, we interpret the lack of observablemagnetite in the inclusions early in the experiments to reﬂect thefact that the crystals are not optically resolvable (B0.5 mm) by themethods used here (including scanning electron microscopy(SEM) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM) in Fig. 2c,d).

tuv.seg: In order for magnetite to become sufﬁciently large that they canbe recognized either optically or during microanalysis, hydrogenmust be lost from the SFI.
Pour que les magnétites deviennent suffisamment grandes pour être reconnues soit optiquement, soit lors de la microanalyse, l'hydrogène doit être perdu dans l'IFS.

The chemical composition of the reactionproducts was inferred from elemental maps obtained from time-of-ﬂight secondary ion mass spectrometry (TOF-SIMS) analysesof the SFI (Fig. 3 and Supplementary Tables 1 and 2).

tuv.seg: As such,the sample was cycled from room temperature to 280 (cid:2)C numerous times during the experiment.Chaixmeca, experiments in a microscope heating stage whereby the SFI were kept at a constant temperature of 280 (cid:2)C and the heating stage was placed onto the Raman microprobe at various timesfor analysis.Mineral assemblages: B, brucite; M, magnetite; S, serpentine.further limited by competition for iron by serpentine and brucite,or by the slow rate at which hydrogen diffuses out of theinclusions29 when (or if) the inclusion approaches equilibrium.Mineral composition.

De ce fait, l'échantillon a été soumis à des cycles de température allant de la température ambiante à 280 (cid : 2) C plusieurs fois au cours de l'expérience. Chaixmeca : expériences dans une platine chauffante au microscope dans lesquelles les IFS ont été maintenues à une température constante de 280 (cid : 2) C et la platine chauffante a été placée sur la microsonde Raman à divers moments pour l'analyse. Assemblages minéralogiques : B, brucite ; M, magnétite ; S, serpentine. La limitation supplémentaire provient de la compétition pour le fer par la serpentine et la brucite, ou de la lenteur de la diffusion de l'hydrogène hors des inclusions29 lorsque (ou si) l'inclusion approche de l'équilibre. Composition minéralogique.

tuv.seg: First, olivine dissolves:formeven in itsis the result of several simultaneous reactionssimplestMg2SiO4 OlðÞ þ 4H þ ¼ 2Mg2 þ þ SiO2ðaqÞ þ 2H2Oð3ÞWhen the concentrations of dissolved species become sufﬁcientlyhigh, serpentine and brucite nucleate and precipitate according to:3Mg2 þ þ 2SiO2ðaqÞ þ 5H2O ¼ Mg3Si2O5 OHðÞ4 SrpðÞ þ 6H þMg2 þ þ 2H2O ¼ Mg OHððÞ2 BrcÞ þ 2H þð4Þð5ÞReactions (4) and (5) consume water and generate hydrogen ionsNATURE COMMUNICATIONS | 8:16107 | DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107 | www.nature.com/naturecommunications3

Tout d'abord, l'olivine se dissout : elle est le résultat de plusieurs réactions simultanées les plus simples Mg2SiO4 OlðÞ þ 4H þ ¼ 2Mg2 þ þ SiO2ðaqÞ þ 2H2Oð3Þ. Lorsque les concentrations d'espèces dissoutes deviennent sufﬁsamment élevées, la serpentine et la brucite se nucléent et se précipitent selon : 3Mg2 þ þ 2SiO2ðaqÞ þ 5H2O ¼ Mg3Si2O5 OHðÞ4 SrpðÞ þ 6H þMg2 þ þ 2H2O ¼ Mg OHððÞ2 BrcÞ þ 2H þð4Þð5Þ. Les réactions (4) et (5) consomment de l'eau et génèrent des ions hydrogène. Communications NATURE | 8:16107 | DOI : 10.1038/ncomms16107 | www.nature.com/naturecommunications3

Eventually, the overall process reaches a steady state wherebythe rates of Mg and SiO2(aq) released from olivine dissolution(reaction 3) are balanced by their rates of consumption byserpentine (reaction 4) and brucite (reaction 5) formation.As the formation of serpentine and brucite consumes H2O, thesalinity of the aqueous solution in SFI increases as the reactionproceeds.

tuv.seg: NATURE COMMUNICATIONS | DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107ARTICLE(H þ ) that in turn promote forsterite dissolution (reaction 3).
NATURE COMMUNICATIONS | DOI : 10.1038/ncomms16107ARTICLE (H þ ) qui à son tour favorise la dissolution de la forstérite (réaction 3).

tu.prop: NATURE COMMUNICATIONS | DOI: 10.1038/ncomms16107ARTICLE(H þ ) that in turn promote forsterite dissolution (reaction 3).

We used the increase in salinity as a proxy for H2Oconsumption and reaction progress, to quantify the reactionrates (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) (Fig. 4a,b, Supplementary Table 3 andSupplementary Data 1 and 2).

tuv.seg: Eventually, the overall process reaches a steady state wherebythe rates of Mg and SiO2(aq) released from olivine dissolution(reaction 3) are balanced by their rates of consumption byserpentine (reaction 4) and brucite (reaction 5) formation.As the formation of serpentine and brucite consumes H2O, thesalinity of the aqueous solution in SFI increases as the reactionproceeds.

Finalement, le processus global atteint un état stable dans lequel les taux de Mg et de SiO2(aq) libérés par la dissolution de l'olivine (réaction 3) sont équilibrés par leurs taux de consommation pour la formation de serpentine (réaction 4) et de brucite (réaction 5). Comme la formation de serpentine et de brucite consomme de l'H2O, la salinité de la solution aqueuse dans les IFS augmente au fur et à mesure que la réaction progresse.

tu.prop: Eventually, the overall process reaches a steady state wherebythe rates of Mg and SiO2(aq) released from olivine dissolution(reaction 3) are balanced by their rates of consumption byserpentine (reaction 4) and brucite (reaction 5) formation.As the formation of serpentine and brucite consumes H2O, thesalinity of the aqueous solution in SFI increases as the reactionproceeds.

The reaction progress is expressedas the extent of reaction (x), deﬁned as the number of moles (n)of reactant consumed or product generated per unit time andnormalized by the stoichiometric coefﬁcient (u) for the phasebeing consumed or produced.

tuv.seg: We used the increase in salinity as a proxy for H2Oconsumption and reaction progress, to quantify the reactionrates (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) (Fig. 4a,b, Supplementary Table 3 andSupplementary Data 1 and 2).

Nous avons utilisé l'augmentation de la salinité comme indicateur de la consommation de H2O et de la progression de la réaction, pour quantifier les taux de réaction (mol m (cid :2) 2 s (cid : 2) 1) (cf. Fig. 4a, b, Tableau complémentaire 3 et Données complémentaires 1 et 2).

Le taux de la réaction est mesurée par l'amplitude (x) qui est le nombre de moles (n) de réactifs consommés ou de produits générés par unité de temps, divisé par le coefficient stœchiométrique (u) de la substance consommée ou produite.

tuv.seg: Therefore, the reaction rate of aspecies i is deﬁned as the time (t) derivative of the extent ofreaction:dxdt¼1uidnidtð6ÞAs such, the reaction rate can be deﬁned in terms of the rate ofchange in the amount of any of the species that participate in thereaction, that is,dxdt¼ (cid:2)12dnFodt¼ (cid:2)13dnWdt¼11dnSrpdt¼11dnBrudtð7Þwhere the subscripts Fo, W, Srp and Bru correspond to the phasesforsterite (olivine), water, serpentine and brucite, respectively.Figure 5a shows the rate of serpentinization as a function of theactivity of water.

Par conséquent, le taux de réaction de l'espèce i est déﬁni comme la dérivée par rapport au temps (t) de l'avancement de la réaction : dxdt ¼ 1uidnidtð6Þ Ainsi, la taux de réaction peut être défini en termes de taux de variation de la quantité de l'une des espèces qui y participent, c'est-à-dire dxdt ¼ (cid :2) 12dnFodt ¼ (cid :2) 13dnWdt ¼ 11dnSrpdt ¼ 11dnBrudtð7Þ où les indices Fo, W, Srp et Bru correspondent aux phases de forstérite (olivine), d'eau, de serpentine et de brucite, respectivement. La figure 5a montre le taux de serpentinisation en fonction de l'activité de l'eau.

The resultsshow that serpentinization rates decrease rapidly with increasingsalinity (or decreasing activity of water), and agree with previousstudies that have reported that dissolution rates of forsterite atlaboratory conditions (25 (cid:2)C and 1 bar) decrease as aH2Odecreases30.

These results document the dramatic effect of salinity, as aproxy for water activity aH2O, on the rate of serpentinization ofolivine and are consistent with previous studies that havesuggested that salinity can have a signiﬁcant effect on dissolutionkinetics31–33 including the dissolution of forsterite at far fromequilibrium conditions30,34.

tuv.seg: The slope of the ﬁtted line representsthe rate of reaction (r).log J ¼ (cid:2) 7:24 (cid:5) 0:03ððlog aH2O (cid:2) 0:0081ðÞ þ 103 (cid:5) 4Þ2Þlog aH2O (cid:2) 3481ð (cid:5) 4Þð8Þwhere (cid:2) 7.24 is the log of the rate constant at 280 (cid:2)C and thecoefﬁcient 103 represents the apparent reaction order of H2O.The serpentinization rates obtained here agree with the rates ofreaction from Wegner and Ernst21 after extrapolating the resultsto unit activity (pure water) with a log J of (cid:2) 7.51 (Fig. 5b).

La pente de la ligne ajustée représente le taux de réaction (r). log J ¼ (cid :2) 7:24 (cid :5) 0:03 ððlog aH2O (cid :2) 0: 0081ðÞ þ 103 (cid :5) 4Þ2Þlog aH2O (cid :2) 3481ð (cid :5) 4Þð8Þ où (cid :2)7.24 est le logarithme de la constante de vitesse à 280 (cid :2)C et le coefficient 103 représente l'ordre de réaction de H2O apparent. Les taux de serpentinisation obtenus ici sont en accord avec les taux de réaction de Wegner et Ernst21 après extrapolation des résultats à une activité unitaire (eau pure) avec un log J de (cid :2) 7.51 (cf. Fig. 5b).

The effect of water activity aH2Oon theconsistent with the generalizedserpentinization reaction given by equation (1), in which thedriving force for production of serpentine and brucite is inverselyproportional to the cube of the activity of H2O and is alsoconsistent with the dissolution of olivine (equation (3)) being therate controlling step of the reaction23.reaction rateisthis requires continualThese results provide a geochemical basis to support thepreviously recognized notion that pervasive serpentinization ofoceanic lithosphere (which is dominated by the mineral olivine)requires open-system behaviour, as imprinted by the seawater-dominated isotope signature commonly observed in serpentinizedperidotites35–38.inﬂuxIn particular,of a lower salinity aqueous ﬂuid (seawater)to dilute theserpentinization ﬂuid and allow serpentinization of olivine tocontinue39.

tuv.seg: These results document the dramatic effect of salinity, as aproxy for water activity aH2O, on the rate of serpentinization ofolivine and are consistent with previous studies that havesuggested that salinity can have a signiﬁcant effect on dissolutionkinetics31–33 including the dissolution of forsterite at far fromequilibrium conditions30,34.

Ces résultats documentent l'effet spectaculaire de la salinité, en tant que proxy de l'activité de l'eau aH2O, sur le taux de serpentinisation de l'olivine et sont cohérents avec des études précédentes qui ont suggéré que la salinité peut avoir un effet significatif sur la cinétique de dissolution31-33, y compris la dissolution de la forstérite dans des conditions loin de l'équilibre30,34.

tu.prop: These results document the dramatic effect of salinity, as aproxy for water activity aH2O, on the rate of serpentinization ofolivine and are consistent with previous studies that havesuggested that salinity can have a signiﬁcant effect on dissolutionkinetics31–33 including the dissolution of forsterite at far fromequilibrium conditions30,34.

Moreover, salinity may be a major rate-limiting factorwhere ﬂuid inﬂux is restricted due to the absence of openfractures and ﬂuid migration proceeds along grain boundaries orwithin nanoscale porosity40–42.

tuv.seg: The effect of water activity aH2Oon theconsistent with the generalizedserpentinization reaction given by equation (1), in which thedriving force for production of serpentine and brucite is inverselyproportional to the cube of the activity of H2O and is alsoconsistent with the dissolution of olivine (equation (3)) being therate controlling step of the reaction23.reaction rateisthis requires continualThese results provide a geochemical basis to support thepreviously recognized notion that pervasive serpentinization ofoceanic lithosphere (which is dominated by the mineral olivine)requires open-system behaviour, as imprinted by the seawater-dominated isotope signature commonly observed in serpentinizedperidotites35–38.inﬂuxIn particular,of a lower salinity aqueous ﬂuid (seawater)to dilute theserpentinization ﬂuid and allow serpentinization of olivine tocontinue39.

L'effet de l'activité de l'eau aH2O sur le taux de réaction de la serpentinisation est cohérent avec la réaction généralisée de serpentinisation donnée par l'équation (1), dans laquelle la force motrice pour la production de serpentine et de brucite est inversement proportionnelle au cube de l'activité de l'eau aH2O et est également cohérent avec la dissolution de l'olivine (équation (3)) étant l'étape limitant de la réaction23. Le taux de réaction est continu. Ces résultats fournissent une base géochimique pour soutenir la notion précédemment reconnue selon laquelle la serpentinisation pervasive de la lithosphère océanique (qui est dominée par l'olivine minérale) nécessite un comportement en système ouvert, comme en témoigne la signature isotopique dominée par l'eau de mer couramment observée dans les péridotites serpentinées35-38. En particulier, cela nécessite un afflux continu de fluide aqueux de faible salinité (eau de mer) pour diluer le fluide de serpentinisation et permettre la poursuite de la serpentinisation de l'olivine39.

tu.prop: The effect of water activity aH2Oon theconsistent with the generalizedserpentinization reaction given by equation (1), in which thedriving force for production of serpentine and brucite is inverselyproportional to the cube of the activity of H2O and is alsoconsistent with the dissolution of olivine (equation (3)) being therate controlling step of the reaction23.reaction rateisthis requires continualThese results provide a geochemical basis to support thepreviously recognized notion that pervasive serpentinization ofoceanic lithosphere (which is dominated by the mineral olivine)requires open-system behaviour, as imprinted by the seawater-dominated isotope signature commonly observed in serpentinizedperidotites35–38.inﬂuxIn particular,of a lower salinity aqueous ﬂuid (seawater)to dilute theserpentinization ﬂuid and allow serpentinization of olivine tocontinue39.

Furthermore, the effect of salinityon the serpentinization process places constraints on theenvironments in which serpentinization is likely to occur onEarth and possibly on other planetary bodies where high salinityﬂuids are believed to exist43–45.

tuv.seg: Moreover, salinity may be a major rate-limiting factorwhere ﬂuid inﬂux is restricted due to the absence of openfractures and ﬂuid migration proceeds along grain boundaries orwithin nanoscale porosity40–42.

De plus, la salinité peut être un facteur majeur limitant la vitesse où l'influx de fluide est restreint en raison de l'absence de fractures ouvertes et la migration de fluide se produit le long des joints de grains ou à l'intérieur de la porosité à l'échelle nanométrique40-42.

tu.prop: Moreover, salinity may be a major rate-limiting factorwhere ﬂuid inﬂux is restricted due to the absence of openfractures and ﬂuid migration proceeds along grain boundaries orwithin nanoscale porosity40–42.

The micro-reactor technique used in this study provides anovel and promising tool to monitor ﬂuid-rock reactions in situand in real time and can be applied to a wide variety of hostminerals, reaction products, temperatures and different startingﬂuid compositions (for example, CaCl2, CO2, SO4 and Al2O3).

tuv.seg: Furthermore, the effect of salinityon the serpentinization process places constraints on theenvironments in which serpentinization is likely to occur onEarth and possibly on other planetary bodies where high salinityﬂuids are believed to exist43–45.

De plus, l'effet de la salinité sur le processus de serpentinisation impose des contraintes sur les environnements dans lesquels la serpentinisation est susceptible de se produire sur Terre et éventuellement sur d'autres corps planétaires où l'on pense que des fluides à forte salinité existent43-45.

tu.prop: Furthermore, the effect of salinityon the serpentinization process places constraints on theenvironments in which serpentinization is likely to occur onEarth and possibly on other planetary bodies where high salinityﬂuids are believed to exist43–45.

tuv.seg: The micro-reactor technique used in this study provides anovel and promising tool to monitor ﬂuid-rock reactions in situand in real time and can be applied to a wide variety of hostminerals, reaction products, temperatures and different startingﬂuid compositions (for example, CaCl2, CO2, SO4 and Al2O3).

La technique du microréacteur utilisée dans cette étude fournit un outil novateur et prometteur pour surveiller les réactions fluide-roche in situ en temps réel et peut être appliquée à une grande variété de minéraux hôtes, de produits de réaction, de températures et de différentes compositions de fluide de départ (par exemple, CaCl2, CO2, SO4 et Al2O3).

tu.prop: The micro-reactor technique used in this study provides anovel and promising tool to monitor ﬂuid-rock reactions in situand in real time and can be applied to a wide variety of hostminerals, reaction products, temperatures and different startingﬂuid compositions (for example, CaCl2, CO2, SO4 and Al2O3).

tuv.seg: Most of the previous experimental studies have used pure water20,21,23,24 and a few others used different ﬂuidcompositions22,23,25.
La plupart des études expérimentales précédentes ont utilisé de l'eau pure20,21,23,24 et quelques autres ont utilisé différentes compositions fluides22,23,25.

tu.prop: Most of the previous experimental studies have used pure water20,21,23,24 and a few others used different ﬂuidcompositions22,23,25.
SFIs were trapped in gem quality (inclusion free) natural olivinecrystals (Fo85) at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar following procedures described by Sternerand Bodnar26, and similar to Bali et al.27.

tuv.seg: MethodsSFI preparation.
Méthodes Préparation de l'IFS.

tu.prop: The crystals were thermally fractured byheating to 700 (cid:2)C in an oven at atmospheric pressure and then quenching bydropping the crystals into cold distilled water.

tuv.seg: SFIs were trapped in gem quality (inclusion free) natural olivinecrystals (Fo85) at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar following procedures described by Sternerand Bodnar26, and similar to Bali et al.27.

Les IFS ont été piégées dans des cristaux d'olivine naturels de qualité gemme (sans inclusion) (Fo85) à 650 °C et 5,5 kbar en suivant les procédures décrites par Sternerand Bodnar26, et similaires à Bali et al.27.

tu.prop: SFIs were trapped in gem quality (inclusion free) natural olivinecrystals (Fo85) at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar following procedures described by Sternerand Bodnar26, and similar to Bali et al.27.

The crystals were dried overnight ina vacuum oven at 110 (cid:2)C. Next, one or two 3–5 mm in diameter crystals of olivinewere loaded into a platinum capsule (2.5 (cid:4) 0.5 mm) together with B100 ml of anH2O-NaCl-MgCl2 ﬂuid (Na/Mg weight ratio of 8/1) with salinities of either 1, 3.5,6 or 10 wt%.

tuv.seg: The crystals were thermally fractured byheating to 700 (cid:2)C in an oven at atmospheric pressure and then quenching bydropping the crystals into cold distilled water.
Les cristaux ont été fracturés thermiquement en étant chauffés à 700 °C dans un four à pression atmosphérique, puis ils ont été refroidis en étant trempés dans de l'eau distillée froide.

tu.prop: The crystals were thermally fractured byheating to 700 (cid:2)C in an oven at atmospheric pressure and then quenching bydropping the crystals into cold distilled water.
The capsules were sealed with an arc welder.

tuv.seg: The crystals were dried overnight ina vacuum oven at 110 (cid:2)C. Next, one or two 3–5 mm in diameter crystals of olivinewere loaded into a platinum capsule (2.5 (cid:4) 0.5 mm) together with B100 ml of anH2O-NaCl-MgCl2 ﬂuid (Na/Mg weight ratio of 8/1) with salinities of either 1, 3.5,6 or 10 wt%.

Les cristaux ont été séchés pendant une nuit dans une étuve sous vide à 110 °C. Ensuite, un ou deux cristaux d'olivine de 3 à 5 mm de diamètre ont été chargés dans une capsule de platine (2,5 x 0,5 mm) avec B100 ml d'un fluide H2O-NaCl-MgCl2 (rapport pondéral Na/Mg de 8/1) avec des salinités de 1, 3,5, 6 ou 10 % en poids.

tu.prop: The crystals were dried overnight ina vacuum oven at 110 (cid:2)C. Next, one or two 3–5 mm in diameter crystals of olivinewere loaded into a platinum capsule (2.5 (cid:4) 0.5 mm) together with B100 ml of anH2O-NaCl-MgCl2 ﬂuid (Na/Mg weight ratio of 8/1) with salinities of either 1, 3.5,6 or 10 wt%.

Platinum capsules wereplaced into cold-seal pressure vessels and held at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar for 30 days,which allowed the olivine host to anneal and trap some of the ﬂuid it was in contactwith as synthetic secondary ﬂuid inclusions that were then used as micro-reactorsin this study.

tuv.seg: The capsules were sealed with an arc welder.
Les capsules ont été scellées grâce à la méthode du soudage à l'arc.

tu.prop: The capsules were sealed with an arc welder.

After the ﬂuid inclusion synthesis, the capsules were cooled to roomtemperature and the crystals removed from the capsules and mounted in thermalcement for cutting (B100 to 200 mm wafers) and polishing.

tuv.seg: Platinum capsules wereplaced into cold-seal pressure vessels and held at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar for 30 days,which allowed the olivine host to anneal and trap some of the ﬂuid it was in contactwith as synthetic secondary ﬂuid inclusions that were then used as micro-reactorsin this study.

Des capsules de platine ont été placées dans des réservoirs pressurisés à basse température et maintenues à 650 °C et 5,5 kbar pendant 30 jours, ce qui a permis à l'olivine hôte de recuire et de piéger une partie du fluide avec lequel elle était en contact sous forme d'inclusions synthétiques de fluide secondaire qui ont ensuite été utilisées comme microréacteurs dans cette étude.

tu.prop: Platinum capsules wereplaced into cold-seal pressure vessels and held at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar for 30 days,which allowed the olivine host to anneal and trap some of the ﬂuid it was in contactwith as synthetic secondary ﬂuid inclusions that were then used as micro-reactorsin this study.
The sizes of the SFIvaried between B1 and 40 mm, with most being B10 mm in diameter.Raman characterization.

tuv.seg: After the ﬂuid inclusion synthesis, the capsules were cooled to roomtemperature and the crystals removed from the capsules and mounted in thermalcement for cutting (B100 to 200 mm wafers) and polishing.

Après la synthèse de l'inclusion fluide, les capsules ont été refroidies à température ambiante et les cristaux ont été retirés des capsules et montés dans du ciment thermique pour la découpe (plaquettes d'environ 100 à 200 mm) et le polissage.

tu.prop: After the ﬂuid inclusion synthesis, the capsules were cooled to roomtemperature and the crystals removed from the capsules and mounted in thermalcement for cutting (B100 to 200 mm wafers) and polishing.

Raman analyses were performed using a JY HoribaLabRam HR (800 mm) spectrometer, 600 grooves per mm grating, slit width of150 mm and the confocal aperture was set at 100 mm. A 514.529 nm (green) LaserPhysics 100S-514 Ar þ laser was used with a 50 mW output power at the sourceand B10 mW at the sample, focused through either a (cid:4) 40 or (cid:4) 100 objective, andan electronically cooled open electrode 1,024 (cid:4) 256 pixel CCD (charge-coupleddevice) detector.

tuv.seg: The sizes of the SFIvaried between B1 and 40 mm, with most being B10 mm in diameter.Raman characterization.
Les tailles de l'IFS variaient entre environ 1 et 40 mm, la plupart ayant un diamètre d'environ 10 mm. Caractérisation Raman.

tu.prop: The sizes of the SFIvaried between B1 and 40 mm, with most being B10 mm in diameter.Raman characterization.

Raman X–Y mapping was conducted using a JY Horiba LabRamHR confocal Raman spectrometer with a 473 nm laser source and a 600 grooves permm grating and a 100 mm confocal aperture and (cid:4) 100 objective.Electron microprobe analyses.

tuv.seg: Raman analyses were performed using a JY HoribaLabRam HR (800 mm) spectrometer, 600 grooves per mm grating, slit width of150 mm and the confocal aperture was set at 100 mm. A 514.529 nm (green) LaserPhysics 100S-514 Ar þ laser was used with a 50 mW output power at the sourceand B10 mW at the sample, focused through either a (cid:4) 40 or (cid:4) 100 objective, andan electronically cooled open electrode 1,024 (cid:4) 256 pixel CCD (charge-coupleddevice) detector.

Les analyses Raman ont été effectuées à l'aide d'un spectromètre JY Horiba LabRam HR (800 mm), d'un réseau de 600 rainures par mm, d'une largeur de fente de 150 mm et d'une ouverture confocale réglée à 100 mm. Un laser LaserPhysics 100S-514 Ar þ de 514,529 nm (vert) a été utilisé avec une puissance de sortie de 50 mW à la source et d'environ10 mW à l'échantillon, focalisé sur un objectif x 40 ou x 100, et un dispositif à transfert de charges à électrode ouverte refroidi électroniquement de 1 024 x 256 pixels.

tu.prop: Raman analyses were performed using a JY HoribaLabRam HR (800 mm) spectrometer, 600 grooves per mm grating, slit width of150 mm and the confocal aperture was set at 100 mm. A 514.529 nm (green) LaserPhysics 100S-514 Ar þ laser was used with a 50 mW output power at the sourceand B10 mW at the sample, focused through either a (cid:4) 40 or (cid:4) 100 objective, andan electronically cooled open electrode 1,024 (cid:4) 256 pixel CCD (charge-coupleddevice) detector.
The composition of the olivine host crystal(Supplementary Table 1) used in the experiments was determined using a CamecaSX-50 electron microprobe (EMPA).

tuv.seg: Raman X–Y mapping was conducted using a JY Horiba LabRamHR confocal Raman spectrometer with a 473 nm laser source and a 600 grooves permm grating and a 100 mm confocal aperture and (cid:4) 100 objective.Electron microprobe analyses.

La cartographie Raman X–Y a été réalisée à l'aide d'un spectromètre Raman confocal JY Horiba LabRam HR avec une source laser de 473 nm et un réseau de diffraction à 600 traits par mm et une ouverture confocale de 100 mm et un objectif x 100. Analyses par microsonde électronique.

tu.prop: Raman X–Y mapping was conducted using a JY Horiba LabRamHR confocal Raman spectrometer with a 473 nm laser source and a 600 grooves permm grating and a 100 mm confocal aperture and (cid:4) 100 objective.Electron microprobe analyses.
Accelerating potential was 15 kV, 20 nAcurrent and 1 mm beam size, using natural and synthetic mineral standards forcalibration.Mineral and chemical characterization.

tuv.seg: The composition of the olivine host crystal(Supplementary Table 1) used in the experiments was determined using a CamecaSX-50 electron microprobe (EMPA).
La composition du cristal hôte d'olivine (cf. tableau supplémentaire 1) utilisé dans les expériences a été déterminée à l'aide d'une microsonde électronique Cameca SX-50 (EMPA).

tu.prop: The composition of the olivine host crystal(Supplementary Table 1) used in the experiments was determined using a CamecaSX-50 electron microprobe (EMPA).
To expose the reaction products in theSFI to obtain SEM images, we used a Helios 600 NanoLab FIB-SEM.

tuv.seg: Accelerating potential was 15 kV, 20 nAcurrent and 1 mm beam size, using natural and synthetic mineral standards forcalibration.Mineral and chemical characterization.

Le potentiel d'accélération était de 15 kV, avec un courant de 20 nA et une taille de faisceau de 1 mm, en utilisant des normes minérales naturelles et synthétiques pour le calibrage. Caractérisation minérale et chimique.

tu.prop: Accelerating potential was 15 kV, 20 nAcurrent and 1 mm beam size, using natural and synthetic mineral standards forcalibration.Mineral and chemical characterization.
Chemicalanalyses of the contents of the SFI after the serpentinization experiments wereconducted using TOF-SIMS with a TOF.SIMS 5 IONTOF instrument46–48.

tuv.seg: To expose the reaction products in theSFI to obtain SEM images, we used a Helios 600 NanoLab FIB-SEM.
Pour exposer les produits de réaction dans l'ISF afin d'obtenir des images MEB, nous avons utilisé un Helios 600 NanoLab FIB-SEM.

tu.prop: To expose the reaction products in theSFI to obtain SEM images, we used a Helios 600 NanoLab FIB-SEM.

At eachstep during the analysis, the ion beam consisting of Bi1energy) was ﬁrst set in the high current bunched mode (20 ns pulse duration,B3.7 pA measured sample current, B1 mm lateral resolution) to determine themasses of interest with high mass resolution.

tuv.seg: Chemicalanalyses of the contents of the SFI after the serpentinization experiments wereconducted using TOF-SIMS with a TOF.SIMS 5 IONTOF instrument46–48.
Les analyses chimiques du contenu de l'ISF après les expériences de serpentinisation ont été menées à l'aide de TOF-SIMS avec un instrument TOF.SIMS 5 IONTOF46-48.

tu.prop: Chemicalanalyses of the contents of the SFI after the serpentinization experiments wereconducted using TOF-SIMS with a TOF.SIMS 5 IONTOF instrument46–48.
The beam was typically raster-þ ion pulses (30 keV ionscanned over areas of 100 (cid:4) 100 mm or 200 (cid:4) 200 mm depending on the region ofinterest.

tuv.seg: At eachstep during the analysis, the ion beam consisting of Bi1energy) was ﬁrst set in the high current bunched mode (20 ns pulse duration,B3.7 pA measured sample current, B1 mm lateral resolution) to determine themasses of interest with high mass resolution.

À chaque étape au cours de l'analyse, le faisceau d'ions constitué d'ions pulsés Bi1 (énergie ionique de 30 keV) a d'abord été réglé dans le mode groupé à courant élevé (durée d'impulsion de 20 ns, courant d'échantillon mesuré d'environ 3.7 pA, résolution latérale d'environ 1 mm) pour déterminer les trajectoires d'intérêt avec une résolution de masse élevée.

tu.prop: At eachstep during the analysis, the ion beam consisting of Bi1energy) was ﬁrst set in the high current bunched mode (20 ns pulse duration,B3.7 pA measured sample current, B1 mm lateral resolution) to determine themasses of interest with high mass resolution.

Then, the beam was set in the burst alignment mode (100 ns pulseduration, B0.03 pA measured sample current, B200 nm lateral resolution) toproduce high spatial resolution maps of the selected regions for all masses ofþinterest.

tuv.seg: The beam was typically raster-þ ion pulses (30 keV ionscanned over areas of 100 (cid:4) 100 mm or 200 (cid:4) 200 mm depending on the region ofinterest.
Le faisceau était généralement des impulsions ioniques raster-þ (ions 30 keV) balayés sur des zones de 100 x 100 mm ou 200 x 200 mm selon la région d'intérêt.

tu.prop: The beam was typically raster-þ ion pulses (30 keV ionscanned over areas of 100 (cid:4) 100 mm or 200 (cid:4) 200 mm depending on the region ofinterest.

To expose the SFI in the olivine hosts at various depths we used an O2sputtering ion beam (1 keV ion energy, B300 nA measured sample current) thatwas raster-scanned over an area of 500 (cid:4) 500 mm and centred over the region ofinterest.

tuv.seg: Then, the beam was set in the burst alignment mode (100 ns pulseduration, B0.03 pA measured sample current, B200 nm lateral resolution) toproduce high spatial resolution maps of the selected regions for all masses ofþinterest.

Ensuite, le faisceau a été réglé dans le mode d'alignement en rafale (pulsation de 100 ns, courant d'échantillon mesuré d'environ 0,03 pA, résolution latérale d'environ 200 nm) pour produire des cartes à haute résolution spatiale des régions sélectionnées pour toutes les masses d'intérêt.

tu.prop: Then, the beam was set in the burst alignment mode (100 ns pulseduration, B0.03 pA measured sample current, B200 nm lateral resolution) toproduce high spatial resolution maps of the selected regions for all masses ofþinterest.
The resulting sputtering rate was calculated at 0.3 nm s (cid:2) 1 by using aWyko NT 9100 optical proﬁlometer to estimate the sputtered crater depth.

tuv.seg: To expose the SFI in the olivine hosts at various depths we used an O2sputtering ion beam (1 keV ion energy, B300 nA measured sample current) thatwas raster-scanned over an area of 500 (cid:4) 500 mm and centred over the region ofinterest.

Pour exposer l'IFS dans les olivine hôtes à différentes profondeurs, nous avons utilisé un faisceau ionique de pulvérisation d'O2 (énergie ionique 1 keV, courant d'échantillon mesuré d'environ 300 nA) qui a été balayé par trame sur une zone de 500 x 500 mm et centré sur la région d'intérêt.

tu.prop: To expose the SFI in the olivine hosts at various depths we used an O2sputtering ion beam (1 keV ion energy, B300 nA measured sample current) thatwas raster-scanned over an area of 500 (cid:4) 500 mm and centred over the region ofinterest.
Thesample was maintained at a base temperature of (cid:2) 100 (cid:2)C at all times during theanalysis by inductive liquid nitrogen cooling to prevent the water from desorbingfrom the exposed SFI.

La vitesse de pulvérisation résultante a été calculée à 0,3 nm s -1 en utilisant un profilomètre optique Wyko NT 9100 pour estimer la profondeur du cratère pulvérisé.

tu.prop: The resulting sputtering rate was calculated at 0.3 nm s (cid:2) 1 by using aWyko NT 9100 optical proﬁlometer to estimate the sputtered crater depth.
A constant current (21 eV electron energy) electron beamwas shot on the sample during data acquisition to reduce sample charging.

The TOF-SIMS provided ratios of thenumber of counts for one mass (isotope) to the number of counts for some otherisotope and these counts were converted into a concentration ratio.

To do this, thecount ratio for Mg þ and Fe þ were converted into concentration ratios using theknown Mg to Fe concentration ratio of the olivine host obtained from EMPA andthe count ratio obtained by TOF-SIMS analyses for this same area of the crystal(see Supplementary Table 2).

tuv.seg: The TOF-SIMS provided ratios of thenumber of counts for one mass (isotope) to the number of counts for some otherisotope and these counts were converted into a concentration ratio.

Le TOF-SIMS a fourni des rapports entre le nombre de comptages pour une masse (isotope) et le nombre de comptages pour un autre isotope et ces comptages ont été convertis en un rapport de concentration.

tu.prop: The TOF-SIMS provided ratios of thenumber of counts for one mass (isotope) to the number of counts for some otherisotope and these counts were converted into a concentration ratio.

tuv.seg: To do this, thecount ratio for Mg þ and Fe þ were converted into concentration ratios using theknown Mg to Fe concentration ratio of the olivine host obtained from EMPA andthe count ratio obtained by TOF-SIMS analyses for this same area of the crystal(see Supplementary Table 2).

Pour ce faire, le rapport de comptage pour Mg + et Fe + a été converti en rapports de concentration à l'aide du rapport de concentration Mg/Fe connu de l'olivine hôte obtenu auprès de l'EMPA et du rapport de comptage obtenu par des analyses TOF-SIMS pour cette même zone du cristal (cf. tableau supplémentaire 2).

tu.prop: To do this, thecount ratio for Mg þ and Fe þ were converted into concentration ratios using theknown Mg to Fe concentration ratio of the olivine host obtained from EMPA andthe count ratio obtained by TOF-SIMS analyses for this same area of the crystal(see Supplementary Table 2).

FPD is a colligative property that depends only on the ratio of the numberof moles of the solute (NaCl–MgCl2 in our experiments) to that of the solvent H2O.Thus, as H2O was transferred from the aqueous solution into product phases, theratio of moles of solute to moles of solvent (H2O) increased and the temperature ofthe triple point decreased.

tuv.seg: With increasing salt concentration, the triplepoint migrates to lower temperature and this effect is referred to as the FPD of thesolution.
Avec l'augmentation de la concentration en sel, le point triple migre vers une température plus basse et cet effet est appelé le FPD de la résolution.

tu.prop: With increasing salt concentration, the triplepoint migrates to lower temperature and this effect is referred to as the FPD of thesolution.
The relationship between the salinity (moles of solutein the solution) and FPD has been determined using data for the ternary systemH2O-NaCl-MgCl2 (refs 49,50).

tuv.seg: FPD is a colligative property that depends only on the ratio of the numberof moles of the solute (NaCl–MgCl2 in our experiments) to that of the solvent H2O.Thus, as H2O was transferred from the aqueous solution into product phases, theratio of moles of solute to moles of solvent (H2O) increased and the temperature ofthe triple point decreased.

Le FPD est une propriété colligative qui ne dépend que du rapport entre le nombre de moles du soluté (NaCl-MgCl2 dans nos expériences) et celui du solvant H2O. Par conséquent, lorsque l'H2O a été transféré de la solution aqueuse dans les phases de produit, le rapport entre les moles de soluté et les moles de solvant (H2O) a augmenté et la température du point triple a diminué.

tu.prop: FPD is a colligative property that depends only on the ratio of the numberof moles of the solute (NaCl–MgCl2 in our experiments) to that of the solvent H2O.Thus, as H2O was transferred from the aqueous solution into product phases, theratio of moles of solute to moles of solvent (H2O) increased and the temperature ofthe triple point decreased.
In our experimental method, we quantify the rates of serpentinization based onthe amount of water removed from the aqueous solution and incorporated intohydrous phases.

tuv.seg: The relationship between the salinity (moles of solutein the solution) and FPD has been determined using data for the ternary systemH2O-NaCl-MgCl2 (refs 49,50).
La relation entre la salinité (moles de solutéine dans la solution) et le FPD a été déterminée à l'aide de données pour le système ternaire H2O-NaCl-MgCl2 (cf. réf. 49,50).

tu.prop: The relationship between the salinity (moles of solutein the solution) and FPD has been determined using data for the ternary systemH2O-NaCl-MgCl2 (refs 49,50).

We use the FPD of the aqueous solution as a proxy for the amountof water removed from solution and the precision of our FPD measurement is±0.1 (cid:2)C. However, some amount of H2O must be removed from the solution andincorporated into hydrous phases before the salinity is increased by an amountsufﬁcient to produce a 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD.

tuv.seg: In our experimental method, we quantify the rates of serpentinization based onthe amount of water removed from the aqueous solution and incorporated intohydrous phases.
Dans notre méthode expérimentale, nous quantifions les taux de serpentinisation en fonction de la quantité d'eau extraite de la solution aqueuse et incorporée dans les phases hydratées.

tu.prop: In our experimental method, we quantify the rates of serpentinization based onthe amount of water removed from the aqueous solution and incorporated intohydrous phases.
The actual amount of H2O thatmust be removed from solution to produce a 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD is also afunction of the starting salinity.

tuv.seg: We use the FPD of the aqueous solution as a proxy for the amountof water removed from solution and the precision of our FPD measurement is±0.1 (cid:2)C. However, some amount of H2O must be removed from the solution andincorporated into hydrous phases before the salinity is increased by an amountsufﬁcient to produce a 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD.

Nous utilisons le FPD de la solution aqueuse comme indicateur de la quantité d'eau retirée de la solution et la précision de notre mesure du FPD est de ±0,1 (cid :2)C. Cependant, une certaine quantité de H2O doit être extraite de la solution et incorporée dans des phases hydratées avant que la salinité ne soit augmentée d'une quantité suffisante pour produire une baisse de 0,1 (cid :2)C du FPD.

tu.prop: We use the FPD of the aqueous solution as a proxy for the amountof water removed from solution and the precision of our FPD measurement is±0.1 (cid:2)C. However, some amount of H2O must be removed from the solution andincorporated into hydrous phases before the salinity is increased by an amountsufﬁcient to produce a 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD.

Thus, for example, if the initial salinity is 1 wt%NaCl (FPD ¼ (cid:2) 0.6 (cid:2)C), B15% of the H2O in the initial ﬂuid must be consumedby hydration reactions to drive the FPD lower by 0.1 (cid:2)C to (cid:2) 0.7 (cid:2)C (correspondingto a salinity of B1.2 wt% NaCl).

tuv.seg: The actual amount of H2O thatmust be removed from solution to produce a 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD is also afunction of the starting salinity.
La quantité réelle de H2O qui doit être extraite de la solution pour produire une baisse de 0,1 °C du FPD dépend également de la salinité de départ.

tu.prop: The actual amount of H2O thatmust be removed from solution to produce a 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD is also afunction of the starting salinity.

However, the same 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD fora starting salinity of 3.5 wt% NaCl requires the consumption of 4.7% of the H2O inthe initial ﬂuid, 6 wt% NaCl initial salinity requires a consumption of 2.6% of H2Oand 10 wt% NaCl initial salinity requires consumption of only 1.4% of the initialH2O, as the FPD is lowered from (cid:2) 6.6 (cid:2)C (10 wt% NaCl) to (cid:2) 6.7 (cid:2)C (10.1 wt%NaCl).

tuv.seg: Thus, for example, if the initial salinity is 1 wt%NaCl (FPD ¼ (cid:2) 0.6 (cid:2)C), B15% of the H2O in the initial ﬂuid must be consumedby hydration reactions to drive the FPD lower by 0.1 (cid:2)C to (cid:2) 0.7 (cid:2)C (correspondingto a salinity of B1.2 wt% NaCl).

Ainsi, par exemple, si la salinité initiale est de 1 % en poids de NaCl (FPD ¼ (cid :2) 0,6 (cid :2)C), B15 % de l'H2O dans le ﬂuide initial doit être consommé par des réactions d'hydratation pour abaisser le FPD de 0,1 (cid :2)C à (cid :2) 0,7 (cid :2)C (correspondant à une salinité de B1,2 % en poids de NaCl).

tu.prop: Thus, for example, if the initial salinity is 1 wt%NaCl (FPD ¼ (cid:2) 0.6 (cid:2)C), B15% of the H2O in the initial ﬂuid must be consumedby hydration reactions to drive the FPD lower by 0.1 (cid:2)C to (cid:2) 0.7 (cid:2)C (correspondingto a salinity of B1.2 wt% NaCl).

Thus, until a sufﬁcient amount of reaction has occurred to consume enoughH2O to lower the FPD by Z0.1 (cid:2)C, in the absence of observed reaction products inthe inclusions we have no way of knowing a priori that the reaction has started and,depending on the starting salinity, the amount of H2O consumed (and, therefore,the amount of reaction that must occur) could require from B1 to 15% of theinitial H2O to be removed from solution.

tuv.seg: However, the same 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD fora starting salinity of 3.5 wt% NaCl requires the consumption of 4.7% of the H2O inthe initial ﬂuid, 6 wt% NaCl initial salinity requires a consumption of 2.6% of H2Oand 10 wt% NaCl initial salinity requires consumption of only 1.4% of the initialH2O, as the FPD is lowered from (cid:2) 6.6 (cid:2)C (10 wt% NaCl) to (cid:2) 6.7 (cid:2)C (10.1 wt%NaCl).

Cependant, la même réduction de 0,1 (cid :2)C de la salinité de départ du FPD de 3,5 % en poids de NaCl nécessite la consommation de 4,7 % de H2O dans le fluide initial, 6 % en poids de salinité initiale de NaCl nécessite une consommation de 2,6 % de H2O et 10 % en poids de salinité initiale de NaCl nécessite une consommation de seulement 1,4 % du H2O initial, car le FPD est réduit de (cid :2) 6,6 (cid :2)C (10 % en poids de NaCl) à (cid :2) 6,7 (cid :2)C (10,1 % en poids de NaCl).

tu.prop: However, the same 0.1 (cid:2)C lowering of the FPD fora starting salinity of 3.5 wt% NaCl requires the consumption of 4.7% of the H2O inthe initial ﬂuid, 6 wt% NaCl initial salinity requires a consumption of 2.6% of H2Oand 10 wt% NaCl initial salinity requires consumption of only 1.4% of the initialH2O, as the FPD is lowered from (cid:2) 6.6 (cid:2)C (10 wt% NaCl) to (cid:2) 6.7 (cid:2)C (10.1 wt%NaCl).
All of the microthermometric data(FPD, salinity and H2O moles consumed) are provided in Supplementary Data 1.

tuv.seg: Thus, until a sufﬁcient amount of reaction has occurred to consume enoughH2O to lower the FPD by Z0.1 (cid:2)C, in the absence of observed reaction products inthe inclusions we have no way of knowing a priori that the reaction has started and,depending on the starting salinity, the amount of H2O consumed (and, therefore,the amount of reaction that must occur) could require from B1 to 15% of theinitial H2O to be removed from solution.

Ainsi, jusqu'à ce qu'une quantité suffisante de réaction se soit produite pour consommer suffisamment d'H2O pour abaisser le FPD de Z 0.1 (cid :2)C, en l'absence de produits de réaction observés dans les inclusions, nous n'avons aucun moyen de savoir a priori que la réaction a commencé et, en fonction de la salinité de départ, la quantité d'H2O consommée (et, par conséquent, la quantité de réaction qui doit se produire) pourrait nécessiter de B1 à 15% de l'H2O initial a extraire de la solution.

tu.prop: Thus, until a sufﬁcient amount of reaction has occurred to consume enoughH2O to lower the FPD by Z0.1 (cid:2)C, in the absence of observed reaction products inthe inclusions we have no way of knowing a priori that the reaction has started and,depending on the starting salinity, the amount of H2O consumed (and, therefore,the amount of reaction that must occur) could require from B1 to 15% of theinitial H2O to be removed from solution.
