tu.prop: All of the microthermometric data(FPD, salinity and H2O moles consumed) are provided in Supplementary Data 1.

To calculate themass of H2O consumed by the serpentinization reaction as a function of the FPD,we constructed a simple mass balance model that relates the mass of H2O (moles)removed from the aqueous ﬂuid during the reaction to the FPD.

Changements de salinité et de H2O dissipé lors de la serpentinisation.

tu.prop: Salinity changes and H2O consumed during serpentinization.

tuv.seg: To calculate themass of H2O consumed by the serpentinization reaction as a function of the FPD,we constructed a simple mass balance model that relates the mass of H2O (moles)removed from the aqueous ﬂuid during the reaction to the FPD.

Pour calculer la masse de H2O consommée par la réaction de serpentinisation en fonction du FPD, nous avons construit un modèle de bilan massique simple qui relie la masse de H2O (mol) extraite du fluide aqueux au cours de la réaction au FPD.

tu.prop: To calculate themass of H2O consumed by the serpentinization reaction as a function of the FPD,we constructed a simple mass balance model that relates the mass of H2O (moles)removed from the aqueous ﬂuid during the reaction to the FPD.

The density of theaqueous solution in the SFI was calculated using the HOKIEFLINCS H2O_NaClprogramme51 and assuming that the system H2O-NaCl provides a reasonableapproximation for the relationship between salinity and density for the ﬂuids in theSFI.

tuv.seg: The total mass of solution in the SFI is given by:ÞÞ rSFIððMSFI¼ VSFIð9Þwhere VSFI is the volume of an ideal spherical ﬂuid inclusion (5.24 (cid:6) 10 (cid:2) 10 cm3)and rSFI is the density of the solution in the SFI (in g cm (cid:2) 3).

La masse totale de la solution dans l'IFS est donnée par : ÞÞ rSFIððMSFI ¼ VSFIð9Þ où VSFI est le volume d'une inclusion idéale de fluide sphérique (5,24 (cid :6) 10 (cid :2) 10 cm3)et rSFI est la densité de la solution dans l'IFS (en g cm (cid :2) 3).

tu.prop: The total mass of solution in the SFI is given by:ÞÞ rSFIððMSFI¼ VSFIð9Þwhere VSFI is the volume of an ideal spherical ﬂuid inclusion (5.24 (cid:6) 10 (cid:2) 10 cm3)and rSFI is the density of the solution in the SFI (in g cm (cid:2) 3).
Accordingly, the densities at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar for salinities of 1, 3.5, 6 and10 wt% NaCl are B0.84, B0.87, B0.88 and B0.90 g cm (cid:2) 3, respectively.

tuv.seg: The density of theaqueous solution in the SFI was calculated using the HOKIEFLINCS H2O_NaClprogramme51 and assuming that the system H2O-NaCl provides a reasonableapproximation for the relationship between salinity and density for the ﬂuids in theSFI.

La densité de la solution aqueuse dans l'IFS a été calculée à l'aide du programme HOKIEFLINCS H2O_NaCl51 et en supposant que le système H2O-NaCl fournit une approximation raisonnable de la relation entre la salinité et la densité des fluides dans l'IFS.

tuv.seg: Accordingly, the densities at 650 (cid:2)C and 5.5 kbar for salinities of 1, 3.5, 6 and10 wt% NaCl are B0.84, B0.87, B0.88 and B0.90 g cm (cid:2) 3, respectively.
En conséquence, les densités à 650 (cid :2)C et 5,5 kbar pour des salinités de 1, 3,5, 6 et 10 % en poids de NaCl sont B0,84, B 0,87, B0,88 et B0,90 g cm (cid :2) 3, respectivement.

Then, theamount of H2O in the SFI after some amount of reaction has occurred to lower theFPD by some measureable amount (corresponding to an increase in salinity) iscalculated.

tuv.seg: First, the mass of H2O in the SFI corresponding to the initialsalinity and measured FPD is calculated according to equation (12).
Tout d'abord, la masse de H2O dans l'ISF correspondant à la salinité initiale et au FPD mesuré est calculée selon l'équation (12).

tu.prop: First, the mass of H2O in the SFI corresponding to the initialsalinity and measured FPD is calculated according to equation (12).

The loss of H2O from the solution to the product phases correspondingto a given change in salinity was then calculated:Mw loss¼Mw;0 (cid:2) Mwð13Þwhere, Mw,0 is the mass of H2O in grams at t ¼ 0 and Mw is the mass of H2Oobtained from equation (9).

tuv.seg: Then, theamount of H2O in the SFI after some amount of reaction has occurred to lower theFPD by some measureable amount (corresponding to an increase in salinity) iscalculated.

Ensuite, la quantité d'H2O dans l'IFS après qu'une certaine quantité de réaction se soit produite pour abaisser le FPD d'une certaine quantité mesurable (correspondant à une augmentation de la salinité) est calculée.

tu.prop: Then, theamount of H2O in the SFI after some amount of reaction has occurred to lower theFPD by some measureable amount (corresponding to an increase in salinity) iscalculated.

The number of moles of H2O consumed nw was thencalculated as:nw¼Mw loss gð Þ(cid:4)1 mol18 gð14ÞFor every initial salinity (1, 3.5, 6 and 10 wt%), the number of moles of H2Oconsumed was ﬁt as a polynomial expression to determine the number of moles ofH2O consumed as a function of the salinity of the SFI.

tuv.seg: The loss of H2O from the solution to the product phases correspondingto a given change in salinity was then calculated:Mw loss¼Mw;0 (cid:2) Mwð13Þwhere, Mw,0 is the mass of H2O in grams at t ¼ 0 and Mw is the mass of H2Oobtained from equation (9).

La perte de H2O de la solution aux phases du produit correspondant à un changement donné de salinité a ensuite été calculée : Mw perte¼Mw ;0 (cid :2) Mwð13Þoù, Mw,0 est la masse de H2O en grammes à t ¼ 0 et Mw est la masse de H2O obtenue à partir de l'équation (9).

tu.prop: The loss of H2O from the solution to the product phases correspondingto a given change in salinity was then calculated:Mw loss¼Mw;0 (cid:2) Mwð13Þwhere, Mw,0 is the mass of H2O in grams at t ¼ 0 and Mw is the mass of H2Oobtained from equation (9).
For every FPD measurementin an individual SFI, the number of moles of H2O leaving the system wascalculated.

Le nombre de moles de H2O consommées, nw, a ensuite été calculé comme suit : nw¼Mw loss gð Þ(cid :4)1 mol18 gð14Þ Pour chaque salinité initiale (1, 3,5, 6 et 10 % en poids), le nombre de moles de H2O consommées a été adoptée comme une expression polynomiale pour déterminer le nombre de moles de H2O consommées en fonction de la salinité de l'IFS.

Reaction progress was followed in severaldozen SFI by monitoring the change in the FPD through time.

Taux de réaction et équation des taux.

tu.prop: Rates of reaction and rate equation.
To follow thereaction progress it was necessary to observe the changes inside the SFI.

tuv.seg: Reaction progress was followed in severaldozen SFI by monitoring the change in the FPD through time.
Les progrès de la réaction ont été suivis dans plusieurs IFS en surveillant l'évolution du FPD au fil du temps.

tu.prop: Reaction progress was followed in severaldozen SFI by monitoring the change in the FPD through time.
As reactionprogressed, the amount of reaction products increased rendering the inclusionopaque and challenging our ability to follow the reaction progress at high extents ofreaction.

tuv.seg: To follow thereaction progress it was necessary to observe the changes inside the SFI.
Pour suivre les progrès de l'action, il était nécessaire d'observer les changements à l'intérieur de l'IFS.

tu.prop: To follow thereaction progress it was necessary to observe the changes inside the SFI.

Moreover, as the H2O inside the SFI is consumed by reaction (1), theinternal pressure and volume, as well as the concentrations and compositionsinside the inclusion, will be modiﬁed from the initial conditions.

tuv.seg: As reactionprogressed, the amount of reaction products increased rendering the inclusionopaque and challenging our ability to follow the reaction progress at high extents ofreaction.

Au fur et à mesure de la progression de la réaction, la quantité de produits de réaction a augmenté, rendant l'inclusion opaque et remettant en question notre capacité à suivre les progrès de la réaction à des degrés élevés de réaction.

tu.prop: As reactionprogressed, the amount of reaction products increased rendering the inclusionopaque and challenging our ability to follow the reaction progress at high extents ofreaction.
To avoid theproblem of visibility and the effects of changing of the ﬂuid compositions andpressure inside the SFI micro-reactors, our observations where analysed using theinitial rate method52.

tuv.seg: Moreover, as the H2O inside the SFI is consumed by reaction (1), theinternal pressure and volume, as well as the concentrations and compositionsinside the inclusion, will be modiﬁed from the initial conditions.

De plus, comme l'H2O à l'intérieur de l'IFS est consommé par la réaction (1), la pression et le volume internes, ainsi que les concentrations et les compositions à l'intérieur de l'inclusion, seront modifiés à partir des conditions initiales.

tu.prop: Moreover, as the H2O inside the SFI is consumed by reaction (1), theinternal pressure and volume, as well as the concentrations and compositionsinside the inclusion, will be modiﬁed from the initial conditions.

Batch experiments usually require data collection thatincludes large extents of reaction to circumvent issues related to modiﬁcations ofthe initial conditions (that is, composition, pH, pressure and so on)53.

tuv.seg: To avoid theproblem of visibility and the effects of changing of the ﬂuid compositions andpressure inside the SFI micro-reactors, our observations where analysed using theinitial rate method52.

Pour éviter le problème de visibilité et les effets du changement de composition des fluides et de la pression à l'intérieur des microréacteurs IFS, nos observations ont été analysées à l'aide de la méthode du taux initial52.

tu.prop: To avoid theproblem of visibility and the effects of changing of the ﬂuid compositions andpressure inside the SFI micro-reactors, our observations where analysed using theinitial rate method52.
Alternatively, the initial rate method uses numerous short-term experiments with asmall number of concentration measurements at equally spaced times.

tuv.seg: Batch experiments usually require data collection thatincludes large extents of reaction to circumvent issues related to modiﬁcations ofthe initial conditions (that is, composition, pH, pressure and so on)53.

Les expériences par lots nécessitent généralement une collecte de données qui comprend de grandes étendues de réaction pour contourner les problèmes liés aux modiﬁcations des conditions initiales (c'est-à-dire la composition, le pH, la pression, etc.)53.

tu.prop: Batch experiments usually require data collection thatincludes large extents of reaction to circumvent issues related to modiﬁcations ofthe initial conditions (that is, composition, pH, pressure and so on)53.
Then, theconcentration data versus time data for each experiment are ﬁtted to a function andthe slope of that function (rate) is extrapolated to time ¼ 0.

tuv.seg: Alternatively, the initial rate method uses numerous short-term experiments with asmall number of concentration measurements at equally spaced times.
Alternativement, la méthode du taux initial utilise de nombreuses expériences à court terme avec un nombre minimum de mesures de concentration à des moments espacés de manière égale.

tu.prop: Alternatively, the initial rate method uses numerous short-term experiments with asmall number of concentration measurements at equally spaced times.
In our experiments, attime zero little or no brucite þ serpentine had formed and no signiﬁcant water wasconsumed.

tuv.seg: Then, theconcentration data versus time data for each experiment are ﬁtted to a function andthe slope of that function (rate) is extrapolated to time ¼ 0.
Ensuite, les données de concentration en fonction du temps pour chaque expérience sont affectées à une fonction et la pente de cette fonction (taux) est extrapolée au temps ¼ 0.

tu.prop: Then, theconcentration data versus time data for each experiment are ﬁtted to a function andthe slope of that function (rate) is extrapolated to time ¼ 0.
The small volume and pressure changes associated with this smallextent of reaction did not affect the result.

tuv.seg: In our experiments, attime zero little or no brucite þ serpentine had formed and no signiﬁcant water wasconsumed.
Dans nos expériences, peu ou aucune serpentine þ brucite ne s'étaient formées et aucune eau significative n'avait été consommée.

tu.prop: In our experiments, attime zero little or no brucite þ serpentine had formed and no signiﬁcant water wasconsumed.

As such, in our experiments, the numberof moles of H2O consumed by the serpentinization reaction with time was foundfrom the change in nw with time:r¼DnwDtð15Þwhere r is the rate of H2O consumption in mol s (cid:2) 1, Dnw is the number of moles ofH2O consumed by the transformation reaction and Dt is the time in seconds.

tuv.seg: The small volume and pressure changes associated with this smallextent of reaction did not affect the result.
Les faibles variations de volume et de pression associées à la faible ampleur de la réaction n'ont pas affecté le résultat.

tu.prop: The small volume and pressure changes associated with this smallextent of reaction did not affect the result.

Thenumber of moles of H2O removed from solution and incorporated into hydrousphases as a function of time (t) for every SFI was ﬁt by a linear regression and therate of reaction was taken as the slope of the regression line53,54 (see Fig. 4).

tuv.seg: As such, in our experiments, the numberof moles of H2O consumed by the serpentinization reaction with time was foundfrom the change in nw with time:r¼DnwDtð15Þwhere r is the rate of H2O consumption in mol s (cid:2) 1, Dnw is the number of moles ofH2O consumed by the transformation reaction and Dt is the time in seconds.

En tant que tel, dans nos expériences, le nombre de moles de H2O consommées par la réaction de serpentinisation avec le temps a été trouvé à partir du changement de nw avec le temps : r¼ DnwDtð15Þ où r est le taux de consommation de H2O en moles (cid :2) 1, Dnw est le nombre de moles de H2O consommées par la réaction de transformation et Dt est le temps en secondes.

tu.prop: As such, in our experiments, the numberof moles of H2O consumed by the serpentinization reaction with time was foundfrom the change in nw with time:r¼DnwDtð15Þwhere r is the rate of H2O consumption in mol s (cid:2) 1, Dnw is the number of moles ofH2O consumed by the transformation reaction and Dt is the time in seconds.

As the reaction occurs at an interface that separates two phases (solid andliquid), the rate expresses how fast a component is transferred to or from thatinterface and the area of the interface must be taken into account as follows:J¼rAð16Þwhere J is the ﬂux of H2O from the liquid phase into the product phases, deﬁned asthe rate of H2O consumption per unit surface area (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1), r is the rate ofH2O consumption in mol s (cid:2) 1 and A is the surface area of a 10 mm spherical ﬂuidinclusion (1.26 (cid:4) 10 (cid:2) 9 m (cid:2) 2) (Supplementary Data 2).

tuv.seg: Thenumber of moles of H2O removed from solution and incorporated into hydrousphases as a function of time (t) for every SFI was ﬁt by a linear regression and therate of reaction was taken as the slope of the regression line53,54 (see Fig. 4).

Ensuite, le nombre de moles de H2O extraites de la solution et incorporées dans les phases hydratées en fonction du temps (t) pour chaque IFS a été déterminé par une régression linéaire et le taux de réaction a été pris comme pente de la ligne de régression53,54 (cf. Fig. 4).

tu.prop: Thenumber of moles of H2O removed from solution and incorporated into hydrousphases as a function of time (t) for every SFI was ﬁt by a linear regression and therate of reaction was taken as the slope of the regression line53,54 (see Fig. 4).
To generate rate equations,rates obtained as described above were converted into rate equations thatsummarize the effects of solution compositions52.

tuv.seg: As the reaction occurs at an interface that separates two phases (solid andliquid), the rate expresses how fast a component is transferred to or from thatinterface and the area of the interface must be taken into account as follows:J¼rAð16Þwhere J is the ﬂux of H2O from the liquid phase into the product phases, deﬁned asthe rate of H2O consumption per unit surface area (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1), r is the rate ofH2O consumption in mol s (cid:2) 1 and A is the surface area of a 10 mm spherical ﬂuidinclusion (1.26 (cid:4) 10 (cid:2) 9 m (cid:2) 2) (Supplementary Data 2).

Comme la réaction se produit à une interface qui sépare deux phases (solide et liquide), le taux exprime la vitesse à laquelle un composant est transféré vers ou depuis cette interface et la surface de l'interface doit être prise en compte comme suit : J¼ rAð16Þ où J est le ﬂux de H2O de la phase liquide dans les phases du produit, défini comme le taux de consommation de H2O par unité de surface (mol m (cid :2) 2 s (cid :2) 1), r est le taux de consommation de H2O en moles (cid :2) 1 et A est la surface d'une inclusion de fluide sphérique de 10 mm (1,26 (cid :4) 10 (cid :2) 9 m (cid :2) 2) (cf. données complémentaires 2).

tu.prop: As the reaction occurs at an interface that separates two phases (solid andliquid), the rate expresses how fast a component is transferred to or from thatinterface and the area of the interface must be taken into account as follows:J¼rAð16Þwhere J is the ﬂux of H2O from the liquid phase into the product phases, deﬁned asthe rate of H2O consumption per unit surface area (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1), r is the rate ofH2O consumption in mol s (cid:2) 1 and A is the surface area of a 10 mm spherical ﬂuidinclusion (1.26 (cid:4) 10 (cid:2) 9 m (cid:2) 2) (Supplementary Data 2).

To compare the serpentinizationrates obtained in this study with the rate data available in the literature, we con-verted the published data20–25,57 into ﬂuxes (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) by applying theShrinking Particle Model52,58.

tuv.seg: Serpentinization rates from the literature.
Taux de serpentinisation d'après la littérature sur le sujet.

tu.prop: This model assumes that all particles are spheres andthe rate of dissolution equals the rate of conversion.

tuv.seg: To compare the serpentinizationrates obtained in this study with the rate data available in the literature, we con-verted the published data20–25,57 into ﬂuxes (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) by applying theShrinking Particle Model52,58.

Pour comparer les taux de serpentinisation obtenus dans cette étude avec les données de taux disponibles dans la littérature, nous avons converti les données publiées 20-25, 57 en ﬂux (moles m (cid :2) 2 s (cid :2) 1) en appliquant le modèle de particules en rétrécissement52, 58.

tu.prop: To compare the serpentinizationrates obtained in this study with the rate data available in the literature, we con-verted the published data20–25,57 into ﬂuxes (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) by applying theShrinking Particle Model52,58.

As such, the conversion rates(olivine to serpentine and brucite, per day from the literature) were converted intoﬂuxes (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) by determining the rate constant following expressions:where a is the conversion rate fraction, kp is the rate constant and t is time (s).1 (cid:2) 1 (cid:2) aðÞ1=3¼kptð19Þk þ ¼R0kpVmð20Þwhere k þ is the ﬂux (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1), R0 is the particle size radius at time zero andVm is the molar volume of olivine.

tuv.seg: This model assumes that all particles are spheres andthe rate of dissolution equals the rate of conversion.
Ce modèle suppose que toutes les particules sont des sphères et que le taux de dissolution est égal au taux de conversion.

tu.prop: This model assumes that all particles are spheres andthe rate of dissolution equals the rate of conversion.

R0 was obtained by using the averaging methodof Tester et al.52,59, which ﬁnds the effective diameter of the grain sizes by thefollowing method:De¼Dmax (cid:2) Dmin(cid:2)ln DmaxDmin(cid:3)ð21Þwhere De is the effective diameter (m), Dmax is the maximum grain size and Dmin isthe minimum grain size diameter.

tuv.seg: As such, the conversion rates(olivine to serpentine and brucite, per day from the literature) were converted intoﬂuxes (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) by determining the rate constant following expressions:where a is the conversion rate fraction, kp is the rate constant and t is time (s).1 (cid:2) 1 (cid:2) aðÞ1=3¼kptð19Þk þ ¼R0kpVmð20Þwhere k þ is the ﬂux (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1), R0 is the particle size radius at time zero andVm is the molar volume of olivine.

En tant que tel, les taux de conversion (olivine en serpentine et brucite, par jour d'après la littérature) ont été convertis en flux (moles m (cid :2) 2 s (cid :2) 1) en déterminant les expressions suivantes de la constante de vitesse : où a est la fraction du taux de conversion, kp est la constante de vitesse et t est le temps (s).1 (cid :2) 1 (cid :2) aðÞ1 =3¼ kptð19Þk þ ¼ R0kpVmð20Þ où k þ est le ﬂux (moles m (cid :2) 2 s (cid :2) 1), R0 est le rayon de la taille des particules au temps zéro et Vm est le volume molaire de l'olivine.

tu.prop: As such, the conversion rates(olivine to serpentine and brucite, per day from the literature) were converted intoﬂuxes (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1) by determining the rate constant following expressions:where a is the conversion rate fraction, kp is the rate constant and t is time (s).1 (cid:2) 1 (cid:2) aðÞ1=3¼kptð19Þk þ ¼R0kpVmð20Þwhere k þ is the ﬂux (mol m (cid:2) 2 s (cid:2) 1), R0 is the particle size radius at time zero andVm is the molar volume of olivine.

tuv.seg: R0 was obtained by using the averaging methodof Tester et al.52,59, which ﬁnds the effective diameter of the grain sizes by thefollowing method:De¼Dmax (cid:2) Dmin(cid:2)ln DmaxDmin(cid:3)ð21Þwhere De is the effective diameter (m), Dmax is the maximum grain size and Dmin isthe minimum grain size diameter.

R0 a été obtenu en utilisant la méthode de calcul de la moyenne de Tester et al.52, 59, qui détermine le diamètre effectif des tailles de grain par la méthode suivante : De¼Dmax (cid :2) Dmin(cid :2)ln DmaxDmin (cid :3) ð21Þ où De est le diamètre effectif (m), Dmax est la taille de grain maximale et Dmin est le diamètre de taille de grain minimale.
